title: podman rootless does not use slirp4netns as default for port mapping
body:
### Issue Description

Hi, 
we want to use podman rootless inside a gitlab pipeline to be able to run TestContainers without docker and rootless.
Therefore I created some projects which use quay.io/podman/stable as base image and start podman in service mode using "podman system service -t 0 tcp:0.0.0.0:2375". I installed maven and java into the image and can run "mvn test" which connects to podman and runs containers. 
The problem now is, that podman doesn´t use slirp4netns if no explicit network mode is set.
I can verify the issues when running "podman run --rm -d -p 8080:80 nginx". A "curl localhost:8080" now does not work. If I use "podman run --rm --network=slirp4netns -d -p 8080:80 nginx" everything works as expected.
Unfortunately TestContainers also uses no explicit network mode when utilizing the docker socket to podman and therefore I cannot access the ports and the test fails. If I explicitly tell TestContainers to use "slirp4netns" as network mode it works again. The problem is that most TestContainers are fixed and I cannot change the nework mode.

Is there a way to configure podman/build custom podman which always uses "--network=slirp4netns"? I already looked into all config files but did not find any solution.

Thanks for your help and support :)

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Run the doppelrittberger/podman-maven:latest image in docker
2. Run "podman run --rm -d -p 8080:80 nginx" -> "curl localhost:8080" does not work
3. Run "podman run --rm --network=slirp4netns -d -p 8080:80 nginx" -> "curl localhost:8080" works


### Describe the results you received

The mapped ports are inaccessible

### Describe the results you expected

The mapped ports should by default be accessible

### Podman in a container

Yes

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

pod created from https://github.com/doppelrittberger/podman-maven/blob/main/Dockerfile using https://github.com/doppelrittberger/fuse-device-plugin/blob/main/fuse-test.yml

### Additional information

Additional information like issue happens only occasionally or issue happens with a particular architecture or on a particular setting

comments.body:
Check the containers.conf file. The podman image defaults to host networking by default so it is expected that the port mapping is not applied.
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/contrib/podmanimage/stable/containers.conf 
You need to switch `netns` to `private`.

Thanks for the quick response :)
That partly did the trick.
When running "podman run ..." it now works but if I use the podman system service tcp socket it fails with
```
Request Failed(Internal Server Error): netavark (exit code 1): Sysctl error: IO Error: Read-only file system (os error 30)
```
Any idea why this fails?

Because the compat socket will default to `bridge` not `slirp4netns`, for `bridge` we need to setup routing, etc... This involves setting sysctls and in a container procfs is read only be default.
If slirp4netns is enough for you you could set `netns = "slirp4nents"` AFAICT.
